stepAnswer: No, because cumulative rainfall generally increases over time, rather than oscillating up and down.
stepTitle: Would a sinusoidal function be a good model for cumulative rainfall over the course of a year? Why or why not?
choices: No, because cumulative rainfall generally increases over time, rather than oscillating up and down.
Yes, because rainfall patterns are seasonal and repeat annually.
Yes, because the amount of rainfall varies throughout the year.
No, because rainfall is too unpredictable to be modeled by any function.
